title: Justice Dept. Won’t Proceed With Trump’s $1.8 Billion Fund, Blanche Says
summary: The acting attorney general told lawmakers that he would leave in place an order forcing the I.R.S. to drop investigations into President Trump, his family and his businesses.

title: Order Shielding Trump Family From I.R.S. Audits Will Remain, Blanche Says
summary: The acting attorney general said the administration was preserving a broad order protecting the president and his family from audits of already filed returns, despite dropping a $1.8 billion payout fund.

title: G.O.P. Works to Jump-Start Immigration Bill After Trump Retreat on Fund
summary: Republicans hoped the acting attorney general’s vow that the administration was “not moving forward” with a fund for people claiming to be victimized by the government would unlock the votes.

title: Trump’s $1.8 Billion Fund Is ‘Not Moving Forward’
summary: Also, California votes on its next governor. Here’s the latest at the end of Tuesday.

title: ‘Did I Have a Choice?’ In New Jersey, Kean Voters Explain Themselves.
summary: Some Republicans acknowledged how strange it felt to vote for Representative Thomas Kean Jr., who has not been seen for months. Mr. Kean issued a statement promising to be transparent about his situation.

title: CBS News Fires Scott Pelley of ‘60 Minutes’
summary: Mr. Pelley, a longtime correspondent and former “Evening News” anchor, harshly criticized the show’s leadership in an extraordinary exchange during a staff meeting on Monday.

title: Supreme Court Clears the Way for Republican-Friendly Map in Alabama
summary: In the first major case since the justices narrowed the Voting Rights Act, the court cleared the way for Alabama to use a map preferred by Republicans that eliminates a majority-Black district.

title: Graham Platner Meets With Senate Democrats Amid Texting Scandal
summary: Mr. Platner, the likely Democratic nominee for Senate in Maine, met in Washington with several senators. Chuck Schumer, the minority leader, dodged questions about his confidence in Mr. Platner.

title: Top Texas Election Official to Resign, Months Before General Contest
summary: Jane Nelson, the Texas secretary of state, was appointed to the role overseeing statewide elections in 2023 by Gov. Greg Abbott.

title: Why the U.S.-Iran Negotiations Are Taking So Long
summary: As negotiations to end the war in Iran drag on, our reporter David E. Sanger describes the factors that are complicating any agreement.

title: War Games and Warnings on Strait of Hormuz Went Unheeded by Trump
summary: Over the past two decades, Iran repeatedly threatened to close down the waterway. President Trump underestimated Iran’s ability to do so.

title: How Three Chess Friends Battled Demons and Saved Two Lives
summary: A homeless chess hustler, a scholar and an aging recluse found hope in a trashed apartment by Central Park.

title: New Proposal Would Allow Administration to Block Grants if They Don’t Support Trump’s Agenda
summary: A new proposal would allow the administration to block grants if they do not satisfy President Trump’s agenda or support what it calls “anti-American” values.

title: Rubio Suggests U.S. Return to Global Vaccine Program in Rebuke of Kennedy
summary: Secretary of State Marco Rubio indicated in pointed testimony to senators that he was reclaiming control of the U.S. relationship with Gavi, an international vaccine alliance.

title: The Toll of a ‘Missing Scientists’ Conspiracy Theory on the Families Left Behind
summary: The claim that their loved ones’ deaths and disappearances are linked is almost certainly false — but the loss remains real.

title: A Stray Dog Became a Cultural Symbol in Brazil. Now, Mexico Is Claiming It.
summary: To Brazilians, the ubiquitous caramel-colored mutts are a national icon. But a Mexican state has adopted them as its own homegrown symbol.

title: Scientists Find Way to Supercharge Dangerous Computer ‘Worms’ With A.I.
summary: Researchers at the University of Toronto showed how hackers could use artificial intelligence to create a program that could target any known flaw in the world’s computers.

title: In Her Memoir, Jill Biden Is a Watchful Spouse Who Didn’t Always Speak Up About Joe Biden
summary: The former first lady’s new book reflects an insular White House where loyalty was prized and President Joseph R. Biden Jr.’s feelings were prioritized over health concerns.

title: Book Review: ‘View From the East Wing,’ by Jill Biden
summary: Beyond a few pointed digs at her husband’s successor, “View From the East Wing” largely sticks to the head-spinning details of first lady-hood.

title: Peabo Bryson, Soul Singer Known as the Voice of Love, Dies at 75
summary: He was admired for impassioned duets with singers like Roberta Flack and Celine Dion and for the Disney hits “Beauty and the Beast” and “A Whole New World.”

title: Carney Says Canada Faces a ‘Crisis of Antisemitism’
summary: Prime Minister Mark Carney’s comments followed calls for action from Jewish organizations and after a government report found that antisemitism had surged in Canada in recent years.

title: ‘La La Land’ Orchestral Performance Saved by Keyboardist in the Audience
summary: After a musician fell ill during a live performance of the score from “La La Land,” the composer Justin Hurwitz asked for a sight reader. A 21-year-old student stepped up.

title: Trump Has Failed as Commander in Chief
summary: Trump has failed to unite the country while at war and instead is seeking personal gain.

title: Dear President Ozymandias
summary: Look on his works and despair.
